@@ -944,7 +944,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.2","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): websocket attach client and lifecycle robustness","description":"Background
- A terminal emulator is not production-usable on the web without robust attach lifecycle logic to connect browser UI with remote PTY streams.
- xterm.js deployments commonly depend on mature websocket glue. FrankenTermJS must ship an equivalent first-party path without legacy baggage.

Goal
- Deliver a deterministic, bounded, and protocol-compliant websocket attach client for FrankenTermJS.

In Scope
- Client state machine from disconnected to connected to teardown.
- Binary protocol envelope codec with strict validation.
- Flow-control windows and explicit backpressure behavior.
- Reconnect and resume semantics with clear guarantees.
- End-to-end harness coverage against ftui-remote bridge.

Out of Scope
- Ad hoc plugin-based attach behavior.
- Best-effort unbounded buffering under overload.

Why This Matters
- Without this, remote mode remains demo-only.
- This track is required for practical replacement of xterm.js in real web apps.

Acceptance Criteria
- Browser attaches to ftui-remote and supports interactive shell workflows under load.
- Protocol errors are deterministic and diagnosable.
- Memory and queue behavior remain bounded during output bursts.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:33.956536552Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.417575547Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","protocol","remote","websocket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:33.956536552Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:03.229894445Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.2.1","title":"task(attach): specify deterministic websocket client state machine","description":"States, transitions, retry classes, and teardown guarantees for attach lifecycle.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Protocol behavior matches websocket specification for covered message types.
- Attach lifecycle behavior is deterministic and test-covered.
- Backpressure behavior stays bounded under burst traffic.
- E2E attach scenarios pass with structured logs.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Attach behavior must be explicit and auditable; avoid hidden retry or buffering behavior.
- Treat protocol compliance as non-negotiable: validate lengths/types strictly and fail deterministically.
- Backpressure handling must prioritize bounded memory over perfect throughput.
- Add test logs that explain state transitions and flow-control decisions for debugging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:38.595867239Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:44:37.051863359Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T07:44:37.051832291Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic attach client state machine in frankenterm-web (explicit states/events/actions, bounded transition JSONL logs), wired wasm lifecycle API methods, documented protocol state machine in docs/spec/frankenterm-websocket-protocol.md, added focused unit tests in crates/frankenterm-web/src/attach.rs, and validated integrated remote path via tests/e2e/scripts/test_remote_resize_storm.sh (pass).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","state-machine","websocket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:38.595867239Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.3","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): scrollback viewport, navigation, and follow-output semantics","description":"Background
- Scrollback and viewport behavior define day-to-day terminal usability.
- Incorrect viewport semantics cause lost context, jumpy UX, and broken search/navigation.

Goal
- Implement robust scrollback/viewport architecture with deterministic user interactions.

In Scope
- Unified model over active grid and scrollback history.
- User navigation APIs and input mappings.
- Follow-output behavior with explicit transitions.
- Resize semantics that preserve intent.
- Search across current viewport plus history.
- Performance validation for large histories.

Out of Scope
- Implicit reflow magic that changes historical text without explicit policy.

Why This Matters
- This is core parity with xterm-class expectations and directly impacts user trust.

Acceptance Criteria
- Large history navigation is smooth and predictable.
- Search and follow-output interactions are stable under continuous output.
- Behavior is documented and fixture-tested.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:34.334301356Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:22:52.278498758Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:22:52.278478751Z","close_reason":"All child tasks completed (bd-2vr05.3.1 through bd-2vr05.3.5): unified viewport model, navigation APIs, follow-output + resize policy, full-history search navigation, and large-scrollback stress/failure-injection coverage landed in frankenterm-web with validation evidence and structured E2E logs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","scrollback","terminal","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:34.334301356Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:03.607263009Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
